algorithms.title: Abdominal Pain Systematic Assessment
algorithms.description: Building a differential for abdominal pain while screening for non-abdominal mimics and metabolic emergencies.
steps: Obtain vital signs and screen for non-abdominal mimics: consider a 12-lead ECG if cardiac risk factors are present, and check glucose.

Localize the pain: periumbilical migrating to RLQ suggests appendicitis; RUQ suggests cholecystitis; LLQ (especially in older patients) suggests diverticulitis; epigastric radiating to the back suggests pancreatitis.
Ask about dialysis status and missed treatments; if present with weakness or paresthesias, screen for hyperkalemia and apply a cardiac monitor.
Assess for dehydration: skin tenting, dry mucous membranes, and history of vomiting/diarrhea/poor intake.
Reassess vital signs and pain character over time; treat supportively and transport.
